text: Okay so I finally tried that ramen place on Fifth.
And? Don't leave me hanging.
Honestly? Best broth I've had all year. No contest.
Ugh, now I'm starving and it's only ten.
Is it supposed to rain tonight?
Yeah, like a ninety percent chance. Bring the umbrella.
Great. I just washed the car, of course.
My train sat in a tunnel for forty minutes this morning.
Forty? Oof. Did they even say why?
Signal problems. It's always "signal problems."
You want the last slice or can I take it?
Take it. I'm stuffed.
You sure? You're eyeing it pretty hard.
Ha! Fine, split it with me.
Mom called. Twice.
Uh oh. Birthday thing?
Worse — she wants us all home for Thanksgiving. All of us.
Even Uncle Ray? This should be interesting.
So what's the plan for Saturday?
Hmm, I was thinking hike in the morning, then... I don't know, tacos?
Tacos after a hike; you're speaking my language.
Did you finish that show yet?
No spoilers! I'm only on episode four.
Episode four? Oh you sweet summer child. Buckle up.
The game last night — did you see that final play?
See it? I was screaming at my TV like a lunatic!
Same. My neighbor actually knocked on the wall.
I think I'm coming down with something.
You do sound stuffy. Tea, soup, bed. Doctor's orders.
You're not a doctor.
I'm a soup expert, which is close enough.
These boots are seventy percent off. Seventy!
Wait, seriously? What's wrong with them?
Nothing! Last season's color, that's all.
Buy them. Buy them right now.
Your dog just stole my sandwich.
Biscuit! Oh no. Oh no, I am so sorry.
It's fine, honestly. He looked... really proud of himself.
The new neighbors are moving in today. Big truck.
Ooh, any first impressions?
They have a piano. And a trampoline. Make of that what you will.
I burned the rice again.
Again? That's three times this week!
The pot hates me, okay? It's personal.
Beautiful morning, huh?
Gorgeous. First frost, though — did you see the grass?
I did. Winter's coming whether we like it or not.
Traffic on the bridge was insane. Bumper to bumper.
That's why I take the ferry now. Twenty minutes, water views, coffee in hand.
Okay, when you put it like that...
Grandpa's telling the fishing story again.
The one with the storm and the broken rod?
That's the one. It gets longer every year.
Last time the fish was, what, four feet?
Free this weekend? There's a farmers market thing.
Saturday I can do. Sunday's family stuff.
Saturday it is. Bring cash — the honey guy doesn't take cards.
That movie was three hours long. Three hours!
Worth it, though. Right?
Eh... two and a half hours worth it, maybe.
Did our team really just lose to the worst team in the league?
Yep. Don't wanna talk about it.
Fair. Same time next week?
Obviously. We're gluttons for punishment.
My back's been acting up since Tuesday.
You lifted those boxes wrong, didn't you.
I lifted them with confidence, which I assumed counted for something.
I need new running shoes but everything's so expensive now.
Check the outlet on Miller Road; I got mine there for half price.
Half? Okay, road trip.
The cat knocked the plant off the shelf again.
Which plant?
The fern. The one I've kept alive for two whole years.
Rest in peace, fern. It had a good run.
Someone keeps parking in front of our driveway.
The gray sedan? That's the guy from number twelve.
Well, number twelve is about to get a very polite note.
Lunch idea: that new falafel cart by the park.
Hmm... how's the line?
Long. But it moves fast, I promise.
Fine, but if it's mediocre, you're buying.
It's snowing! Actual snow!
No way. In October?
Come look out the window before it stops!
My sister got a parrot.
A parrot? On purpose?
On purpose. It already says "absolutely not" and I'm afraid.
How was the drive up?
Long. Two hours of roadwork on the interstate.
Ouch. Well, dinner's hot and there's pie, so.
Pie fixes everything. Everything.
I signed us up for the 5K.
You did what now?
It's for charity! And it's mostly downhill... probably.
"Probably," she says.
Do we have anything for dinner or is it cereal night?
There's leftover curry, some eggs... half a sad bell pepper.
Curry it is. The pepper can wait for tomorrow's sadness.
The doctor said it's just a sprain.
Oh thank goodness. So no cast?
No cast. Just a brace and no soccer for a month.
A month? You're gonna be unbearable.
Look at this sky. Not a single cloud.
Mm. Days like this I almost forgive August.
Almost?
It's still ninety degrees, so yes — almost.
They discontinued my favorite cereal.
Which one, the cinnamon thing?
The cinnamon thing! I'm devastated. Genuinely.
My carpool buddy quit, so now I'm driving alone.
Take the express lane at least?
Can't — that's the whole point of a carpool buddy.
Right, right. Want a very quiet passenger three days a week?
Dad's trying to text with voice commands now.
Oh no. How's that going?
He sent me "purchase the ducks at seven" and I still don't know what he meant.
Farmers market haul: peaches, sourdough, and way too much cheese.
There's no such thing as too much cheese.
The receipt disagrees with you. Loudly.
Are you watching the match tonight or should I not bother asking?
Watching. Wings are already ordered.
Save me two. No — four.
I've been sleeping terribly all week.
Coffee after three again?
...Maybe.
There it is. There's your answer.
The mall was a zoo today.
Holiday shopping already? It's barely November.
Tell that to the guy who elbowed me over a waffle iron.
Our neighbor's band practices at midnight. Midnight!
Are they at least good?
That's the worst part — they're terrible. Passionately terrible.
I taught the dog to shake. Watch this.
Go on then, impress me.
Shake! Shake... okay, he did it earlier, I swear.
Sure he did. Sure.
Soup or salad? Be honest.
It's forty degrees out. Soup. Obviously soup.
Thank you! My point exactly.
The forecast says heat wave through Friday.
Again? We just had one!
Yep. Fill the kiddie pool, we're going full suburban.
I missed my stop because of a podcast.
Which podcast is worth an extra twenty minutes of bus?
The one about the missing violin. I couldn't just leave mid-episode!
My aunt's teaching me her dumpling recipe this weekend.
The secret one? The one she guards with her life?
That very one. I had to swear an oath. Sort of.
Bring me some or the oath means nothing.
Movie night: comedy or something scary?
After the week I've had? Comedy. Nothing with jump scares.
Deal. Popcorn's on me.
I think the cat's mad at me.
Cats are always mad. What'd you do?
I moved her chair. Six inches.
Six inches?! You monster.
Any luck with the jacket you wanted?
Sold out in my size everywhere. Everywhere!
Check the resale apps; that's how I found mine.
Huh — hadn't thought of that. On it.
The bus driver waited for me today. Actually waited!
Wow, small miracles.
I nearly cried. It's been that kind of week.
We're doing a family reunion in June. All forty of us.
Forty?! Where do you even put forty people?
Cousin Dee's farm. Pray for the farm.
The pharmacy called; your prescription's ready.
Oh good. Can you grab it? I'm stuck on a call.
Sure — need anything else while I'm out?
Chocolate. Don't ask, just... chocolate.
Guess who got bumped to first class.
Shut up. You?
Me! Free upgrade. Warm nuts and everything.
The gym was packed at six a.m. Six!
New Year's crowd. Give it three weeks.
You're terrible. Accurate, but terrible.
Do you smell that? Someone's grilling.
Mm, it's the Hendersons. They grill every Friday.
We need to become their best friends. Immediately.
I dropped my phone in the pasta water.
You... how? How does that even happen?
I was draining and texting. Don't lecture me, I've suffered enough.
The rice trick. Go, now.
Wanna split a cab to the airport Sunday?
What time's your flight?
Six forty. So leaving at, like, four?
Four in the morning?! ...Fine. But you're buying coffee.
My tomatoes finally turned red!
The ones you've been guarding since May?
The very same. First harvest tonight — you're invited.
Hey, quick one — do we need milk?
We have milk. We need eggs, though. And maybe bread.
Eggs, bread, got it. Anything else before I hit checkout?
The kids want a bounce house for the party.
How much could a bounce house even cost?
Three hundred. For four hours.
For four... okay, we're renting a bounce house, aren't we.
I walked past our old apartment today.
Aw, the one with the leaky ceiling?
The one with the leaky ceiling and the perfect sunsets. I miss it a little.
It's freezing in here. Who touched the thermostat?
Not me. Ask your son, the polar bear.
He set it to sixty again? Sixty!
The barista knows my order now. I've made it.
Ha! Small-town famous.
She started making it when I walked in. When I walked in!
Okay, that is genuinely elite status.
Did you hear back about the marathon lottery?
Got in. Somehow, I actually got in.
That's amazing! And also — your poor knees.
My knees found out this morning. They're not speaking to me.
What's that noise? Is that the dishwasher?
It's been doing that clunk thing since Monday.
Since Monday?! And we're just... living with it?
I made too much chili. Like, way too much.
Define "way too much."
Nine servings. It's just me here.
Chili freezes beautifully. Also, hi, I'm free tonight.
Cold's finally gone. I can taste food again!
First real meal — what is it?
Pho. Extra everything. Don't try to stop me.
My brother's team made the playoffs.
The rec league team? The Mighty Ducks knockoffs?
Hey, the Thundersticks are a serious organization now.
The Thundersticks. Sure. Serious.
There's a stray cat living under our porch.
Have you fed it?
...Once. Okay, four times.
Congratulations, you have a cat.
Ugh, the elevator's out again.
Sixth floor, huh. My condolences.
My calves will be magnificent by spring; that's what I keep telling myself.
Grandma wants to learn video calls before the holidays.
Aw, that's sweet. Who's teaching her?
Me, apparently. Wish us both luck.
The sequel comes out Friday. FRIDAY!
You've mentioned it. Nine times.
And I'll mention it nine more. Midnight showing — in or out?
In. Obviously in. Was that ever a question?
I tried oat milk in my coffee today.
The verdict?
Weirdly... great? Don't tell my dairy farmer uncle.
Rain again. Third weekend in a row.
The garden loves it, at least.
The garden can share. I had plans!
My knee's clicking when I climb stairs.
Clicking or hurting?
Just clicking. Like a tiny metronome.
Get it looked at anyway. Tiny metronomes turn into big problems.
They put a coffee shop in the old bank building.
The one with the marble columns? Fancy.
You order lattes at the teller windows. It's ridiculous and I love it.
Can you drop the kids at practice? I'm stuck at the dentist.
Yeah, no problem. Four thirty, right?
Four fifteen — coach moved it. Thank you, you're a lifesaver.
I found my old jersey in the attic.
Does it still fit?
We're not going to discuss that.
It doesn't fit.
This heat is unreal. The car thermometer said 104.
A hundred and four?! Stay inside, drink water, be a lizard.
Already on it. I've moved into the basement, basically.
The book club picked a 700-page novel.
Seven hundred? Who's got that kind of time?
Nobody! We're all going to show up having read the summary, and we all know it.
Puppy update: he learned stairs today.
Up or down?
Up. Down is still... a controlled tumble.
Please tell me there's video.
Was that thunder?
Sounded more like the truck next door.
No — there! That's thunder. Grab the laundry off the line!
My cousin's wedding is now a destination wedding.
Where to?
Portugal. In February.
Well. Guess you're going to Portugal in February.
The soup needs something. Taste it.
Hmm... salt? No, wait — lemon. It needs lemon.
Lemon! Yes. You're hired.
I keep seeing the same guy on my commute. We nod now.
A nod friend! That's a big step.
Next month we might attempt words. Baby steps.
Half-price sushi after eight. Just saying.
Half-price sushi is how you get stories that start with "so I was in the ER."
It's a good place! I've been three times!
Three times and how many stories?
Dad fixed the fence. Sort of.
Define "sort of."
It stands. It leans, but it stands.
I got tickets to the game — third row!
Third row?! How?
Retirement gift from Joe's brother-in-law. Long story. You in?
I was in before you finished the word "tickets."
The pediatrician said she's in the ninetieth percentile for height.
Ninetieth! She gets that from your side.
Clearly. My side needed a stool for the top shelf since forever.
Someone's practicing trumpet in the courtyard.
At seven a.m.? Bold.
It's kind of beautiful, though. Terrible, but beautiful.
I returned the blender. Finally.
The one from the wedding? That was two years ago!
Store credit doesn't expire. That's the lesson here.
What do you feel like — pizza or the leftover lasagna?
Is that even a question? Pizza.
The lasagna will remember this betrayal.
The lasagna's been in there since Sunday; it betrayed us first.
My sister's teaching yoga now. Certified and everything.
Good for her! Have you taken her class?
Once. I pulled something reaching for "inner peace."
The dog barked at the vacuum for a solid hour.
An hour? What's his endgame?
There is no endgame. There is only the war.
The vacuum always wins, buddy. Always.
Frost warning tonight. Cover your tomatoes.
It's mid-May! This is illegal.
Tell it to the sky. Old sheets work fine, by the way.
I finally beat my brother at chess.
No! After how many years?
Twenty-two years. He demanded a rematch immediately.
Of course he did. Did you accept?
The bakery sells day-old croissants for a dollar.
A dollar?! Since when?
Since forever, apparently. We've been paying full price like fools.
My throat's scratchy. Don't come too close.
Zinc, honey-lemon, and cancel your Thursday thing.
But the Thursday thing is the fun thing...
Scratchy throat says otherwise. Rest up.
Neighbors invited us over for a barbecue Sunday.
The new ones? With the piano?
And the trampoline, yes. I'm bringing potato salad.
Make the good one — first impressions matter.
I can't find my keys and we leave in five minutes.
Jacket pocket. The blue one.
...How do you always know?
Because it's always the jacket pocket. Always.
They're repaving our street starting Monday.
For how long?
Three weeks. We park two blocks over till August, basically.
I watched a documentary about octopuses last night.
And now you love octopuses.
And now I love octopuses. They have nine brains! Nine!
You were one documentary away from this your whole life.
Morning. Coffee's still hot if you want it.
Bless you. Rough night — the baby was up at three.
Again? Go sit; I'll handle breakfast.
The lake's frozen enough to skate on!
Says who?
Says the parks department, officially. Grab your skates!
I might've overwatered the cactus.
The cactus. The plant famous for not needing water.
It looked thirsty!
We still on for trivia night?
Seven sharp. And we're not doing the sports category this time.
One bad night and suddenly—
Nineteen points, Marcus. We scored nineteen points.
Someone ate my labeled yogurt from the fridge.
The one that said "do not touch" in red marker?
In red marker! With underlines!
How's your mom doing after the surgery?
Really well, actually. She's already bossing the nurses around.
Ha, that's how you know she's fine.
It's sweater weather. Finally, officially, sweater weather.
You've been waiting since July, haven't you.
I have a drawer ready. A whole drawer.
The umpire blew that call and you know it.
It was strike three! Right down the middle!
It was outside by a mile, and I'll die on this hill.
Then die on it — replay's on the screen right now.
Quick — window seat or aisle?
Aisle. Always aisle.
Wrong. The window has clouds. Case closed.
My grandmother's quilt won a ribbon at the fair!
The blue one with the stars?
First place! She's acting humble, but she called everyone she knows.
The vet says Milo needs to lose two pounds.
Two pounds on a cat is a lot, right?
It's like twenty for us. No more table scraps — I mean it this time.
You said that last time, right before the bacon incident.
I hate parallel parking. Just circling the block forever.
There's a garage on Second, you know. Three bucks an hour.
Three bucks buys my dignity? Sold.
We ran out of candy an hour into trick-or-treating.
An hour! How many kids came through?
Hundreds. It was beautiful chaos. Next year: double the candy.
I think the fish is done. Does that look done to you?
Poke it. Does it flake?
It... flakes-ish?
Two more minutes. Trust the flake.
My commute's down to eleven minutes with the new bike route.
Eleven! What was it before?
Forty, on a good bus day. I'm never going back.
Aunt Rosa's bringing her famous flan to dinner.
Famous good or famous bad?
You've never had the flan? Oh, you're about to have a religious experience.
The weather app says sun; the sky says otherwise.
Trust the sky. Take the jacket.
The sky it is.
Bowling Friday — you, me, and the Hendersons.
The Hendersons who grill or the Hendersons with the boat?
The grill ones. Obviously the grill ones.
Then yes. And ask them to bring leftovers.
The pharmacy switched my pills to a generic.
Same thing, way cheaper. Usually.
That's what the pharmacist said too — like word for word.
Because it's true, word for word.
Little league starts Saturday and guess who's coaching.
No. You?
Me! Coach Dad, reporting for duty. I bought a whistle.
A whistle. Heaven help those children.
This peach is perfect. Like, perfect perfect.
From the market or the store?
Market. The store ones are basically baseballs.
Somebody's car alarm has been going for twenty minutes.
The silver hatchback? That's Dale's. He's on vacation.
Of course he is. Of course.
I'm doing dry January.
It's the ninth.
I'm doing dry rest-of-January.
Bold revision. Respect.
The twins learned to whistle. Both of them. Same day.
That house must be a delight right now.
It's a bird sanctuary of chaos, and honestly? Kind of adorable.
Should we walk or drive to the fireworks?
Walk. Parking will be a nightmare.
Walk it is. Bug spray's by the door.
My fantasy team is falling apart.
Injuries or bad picks?
...Both. Mostly bad picks. Don't gloat.
I would never gloat. Out loud.
There's a farmers market opening near the station!
Wait, the empty lot? They finally did something with it?
Opening day's Sunday. First fifty people get free bread.
I fell asleep during the movie. Twice.
The action movie? How?
Comfy chair, big dinner... it was inevitable, really.
Mrs. Patel next door dropped off diwali sweets.
Oh, the little milky ones? Those are incredible.
There were twelve. There are... fewer now.
You ate them all, didn't you.
My son wants a snake. A snake!
How old is he now, nine?
Nine and very persuasive. He made a slideshow.
A slideshow?! Oh, you're getting a snake.
The heat's out in the office again.
Space heater under the desk. It's the only way.
Mine got confiscated. "Fire hazard," they said. Cowards.
I bought a bread machine.
You said, and I quote, "bread machines are for quitters."
The bread changed my mind. It's so good, Sam. So good.
Are we too old for the water park?
Absolutely not. When?
Saturday. Wear the sunscreen this time — all of it.
My daughter beat me at one-on-one. Fair and square.
How old is she?
Thirteen. Thirteen! Her crossover is a crime.
Kids grow up so fast. And apparently so does their handle.
The corner store started stocking the good hot sauce.
The one from your trip? The green one?
The green one! I bought four bottles. Judge me all you want.
Rain check on lunch? My sister just went into labor!
Oh my gosh, GO! Go go go!
Going! I'll text you from the hospital!
This crossword is impossible. Seven letters, "kitchen gadget."
Blender? No wait — spatula?
Spatula fits! You genius.
One down, forty-two to go.
I've got a coupon for that steakhouse. Expires tonight.
Tonight? It's already seven.
So we leave in ten minutes. Steak waits for no one.
The baby said "dog" before "mama."
Ha! The dog is thrilled, I assume?
The dog has never cared less about anything.
Classic dog.
Whose leftovers are these? They smell incredible.
Mine, and don't even think about it.
One bite. One diplomatic bite.
You have your own shelf, use it!
I organized the garage. The whole thing. All day.
Wow — can we finally park a car in there?
Let's not get carried away.
Ice on the steps this morning. Nearly went down.
That's it, I'm buying salt today.
Buy two bags. It's supposed to freeze all week.
My office moved to the fourteenth floor. The view!
Better than staring at the parking structure?
I can see the river. The actual river! I take breaks just to look.
Grandpa wants everyone to wear matching shirts at the reunion.
Matching... shirts. For forty people.
Lime green. He already ordered them.
Lime green. Well. There goes my dignity in every photo forever.
The dentist said no cavities!
First time in how long?
We don't need to put a number on it. We celebrate, we move on.
Storm knocked the power out on Elm Street.
Just Elm? We're two blocks over.
Just Elm. Karen's fridge is toast though — we should offer our freezer.
I'm learning to make my dad's biryani.
The one from every birthday ever?
That one. Attempt number three is tonight.
Third time's the charm — save me a plate either way.
Half marathon's in six weeks and my longest run is four miles.
Four... out of thirteen.
The math is not encouraging, I'm aware.
Run tomorrow. And the day after. I'll bike alongside.
That new bakery does a chocolate babka that'll ruin all other bread for you.
Bold claim.
Come Saturday morning. Bring cash and low expectations for the rest of your bread life.
The kids built a fort out of every cushion we own.
Every cushion?
Every. Cushion. I'm sitting on a wooden chair like it's the 1800s.
Do NOT knock it down. Forts are sacred.
Did you take the trash out?
It's Tuesday. Pickup's Thursday.
It's Wednesday, actually.
...I'll take the trash out.
This coffee tastes like burnt regret.
You let it sit on the burner for three hours.
Details.
The pool opens Memorial Day weekend.
And the line will be around the block, like always.
Worth it. First cannonball of the summer is sacred.
I can't believe he ate the whole pie.
The whole pie? The birthday pie?
Every crumb. He left the plate in the sink like evidence.
You look exhausted. Late night?
Puppy. Three a.m. Squirrel emergency, apparently.
There was no squirrel, was there.
There is never a squirrel.
Should I text him back or wait?
How long's it been?
Twenty minutes.
Text him back, you weirdo. It's your brother.
The library's doing a bake sale Saturday.
Say less. What time?
Nine. The good stuff goes by nine thirty, fair warning.
New phone or fix the cracked screen?
How cracked are we talking?
Spiderweb. Full spiderweb.
New phone. That thing's a hazard.
Grandma's hip surgery got moved up to Monday.
Monday? That's good news, right?
Really good. She'll be dancing at the wedding after all.
It's too quiet in there. What are the kids doing?
Coloring, they said.
Coloring WHAT is my question.
...I'll go check.
I found parking right in front. Right in front!
On a Saturday? Downtown?
I know! I almost took a photo.
The recipe says "season to taste." What does that even mean?
It means you're on your own, chef.
I need numbers! Teaspoons! Guidance!
My allergies are destroying me today.
The cottonwood's out. It's like snow out there.
It's beautiful and I hate it so much.
He proposed! At the taco truck!
At the — wait, THEIR taco truck? Where they met?
Yes! She cried into her al pastor. It was perfect.
Are you seriously wearing shorts? It's forty degrees.
My legs don't get cold.
That's not a thing.
And yet — here I am. Not cold.
The couch won't fit through the door.
Did you measure?
I eyeballed it.
You eyeballed a couch.
Movie starts in twenty. You ready?
Almost — can't find my left shoe.
It's on the stairs. It's always on the stairs.
My sourdough starter died while we were away.
Oh no, not Gerald.
Gerald deserved better. Moment of silence, please.
The kids' school is doing a walkathon fundraiser.
How many laps are we sponsoring?
At a dollar a lap? Knowing Maya... we should set a cap.
I saw your old roommate at the grocery store.
Kevin? How is he?
Still talking about his screenplay. Twenty minutes in the cereal aisle.
Hot or iced?
It's November.
That's not an answer. Some of us are iced-coffee-year-round people.
Hot. Like a normal person. In November.
The game got rained out.
Nooo. Rescheduled or refunded?
Rescheduled — but to a Wednesday afternoon. Who can go then?
I finally cleaned out the junk drawer.
Find anything good?
Three dead batteries, a mystery key, and forty cents.
The mystery key stays. That's the rule.
Aunt Carol's bringing her new boyfriend to dinner.
The race car guy or the beekeeper?
New new. This one paints birds.
Carol contains multitudes.
My step counter says I did nine miles today.
At the mall?
At the mall. Holiday shopping is cardio and no one can tell me otherwise.
The neighbor's rooster starts at 4:45. Every day.
You live downtown. Why does he have a rooster?
That's the question we're all asking, believe me.
Split the check or take turns?
Turns. And it's yours this time.
Is it? Hmm, I distinctly remember—
It's yours. I have receipts. Literal receipts.
The doctor wants me to cut back on salt.
Ouch. The chips too?
The chips too. Pray for me.
First day of school photos — did you see what he wore?
The dinosaur costume?!
He negotiated hard. We settled on the dinosaur backpack.
Diplomacy at its finest.
Is the market open on Sundays now?
Til noon, yeah.
Perfect — pancake ingredients acquired at dawn, then.
They're tearing down the old cinema.
The one with the sticky floors and the broken seat?
Row F, seat twelve. I'm weirdly emotional about it.
We had first dates there. You're allowed to be emotional.
The wifi's out and the kids are... reading?
Do not touch the router. Don't even look at it.
The modem stays broken until further notice. Agreed.
I made your mom's meatball recipe.
And?
The kitchen's a disaster and I regret nothing.
Ferry or the bridge this time?
Ferry. Bridge traffic on a Friday? Never again.
Ferry it is. Window seats, top deck.
He's been in the bathroom taming his hair for forty minutes.
The school dance is tonight, isn't it.
First one. Should I say something?
Not a word. Just have the camera ready.
I signed up for a pottery class.
You? Arts and crafts?
The instructor said I have "promising hands." So there.
Promising hands. I'm framing that.
The furnace guy can't come till Thursday.
Thursday?! It's freezing!
Blankets, soup, and the fireplace. We're pioneers now.
Two-for-one smoothies at the new juice place.
Catch?
You have to follow them online and do a little dance. Kidding — no catch.
Don't scare me like that.
My mom joined a pickleball league.
Everyone's mom joined a pickleball league.
She's ranked now. Like, officially ranked. There's a ladder.
The tide's out — beach walk?
Give me five minutes and a thermos.
Thermos of what?
Cocoa. It's a cocoa kind of morning.
I dropped the casserole. The whole casserole.
Oh NO. The one for the potluck?
Face down. On the one clean patch of floor, which I guess is something.
Grocery store deli. No one has to know.
We got the campsite! The one by the falls!
The one that books out six months ahead?
Set your alarm, I did the impossible. July tenth, three nights.
Why is there a drum kit in the living room?
It was free. On the curb. FREE.
That's how they get you.
That's how they got me.
My glasses prescription changed again.
Stronger?
Stronger. The eye chart and I are no longer friends.
Someone returned our lost dog flyer... with the dog.
WHAT. Buster's home?!
Buster's home! Three streets over the whole time, living his best life at some retiree's house.
That absolute traitor. I'm so relieved.
Toast fell butter-side up this morning.
That's a good omen if I've ever heard one.
Right? Buying a lottery ticket at lunch.
The inlaws land at six. House status?
Kitchen's clean, guest room's ready, hallway is... a work in progress.
Shove it all in the office closet — go go go!
I taught grandma to text and now she sends me memes.
Good memes?
Better than yours, honestly.
That's a low bar and also, rude.
The bike tire's flat again.
Same one? That's the third time.
Third time. There's got to be glass in the tread somewhere.
Snowday! They just called it!
The kids are going to lose their minds.
The kids? I'm making cocoa and building the fort myself.
Did you water the plants?
The real ones or the one we found out was plastic?
This line hasn't moved in ten minutes.
It's the guy paying in coins. I can hear it from here.
Windy enough for you?
My hat's in the next county by now. So yes.
The lasagna needs, what, twenty more minutes?
Fifteen. And stop opening the oven!
I'm supervising.
He walked! Three whole steps!
And I missed it?! Tell me someone got video.
Grandma got it. All eleven seconds, sideways.
Are these your socks on the lamp?
...There's a story.
There's always a story.
The bus was early. EARLY.
That's not possible. That's never happened in recorded history.
I'm thinking soup for dinner. Thoughts?
Only if there's grilled cheese involved.
Was that ever in doubt?
The Hendersons got a boat.
The grill Hendersons?!
The grill Hendersons. Summer just got interesting.
My horoscope said avoid big decisions today.
You were deciding between two sandwiches.
And I panicked! Thanks a lot, stars.
Score check?
Down by four, two minutes left.
I can't watch. Tell me when it's over.
The dog groomer gave him a bow tie.
Please tell me he's still wearing it.
He won't let us take it off. He knows he looks distinguished.
Ants. In the kitchen. Again.
Where this time?
The honey shelf. They have excellent taste, I'll give them that.
I ran into your gym teacher from high school.
Mr. Dobbs? He's still alive?
Alive and still doing push-ups, apparently. He asked about your mile time.
Leftover cake for breakfast — yes or no?
It has eggs and flour. That's basically a muffin.
This is why we're friends.
The car's making that noise again.
The click-click or the whirrr?
Both. It's a duet now.
Rain's coming sideways out there!
Sideways?! The umbrella's useless then.
The umbrella died in the parking lot. It's gone, Jim.
My niece is selling cookies. How many boxes do I put you down for?
What are my options?
Thin mints or the answer is thin mints.
Four boxes.
The recipe calls for saffron. Who owns saffron?
It's like thirty dollars a pinch!
Turmeric. We're using turmeric and nobody will know.
Is it too early for holiday music?
It's November first.
So... yes or no?
Play it. Live your truth.
I got a library card today.
You've lived here nine years.
And the card is nine years overdue. Poetic, really.
The kids want pancakes shaped like dinosaurs.
Can you even do that?
We're about to find out together.
Someone complimented my parallel parking today.
Frame that moment. That's a once-in-a-life event.
Ugh, my phone's at two percent.
There's a charger in the glovebox.
You're the reason this marriage works.
The lettuce went bad already.
We bought it Sunday!
Lettuce lives by its own rules.
Did the package come?
The porch was empty at noon.
The app says "delivered." Delivered WHERE is the question.
My cousin's food truck got reviewed in the paper!
Good review?
Three and a half stars — "the brisket alone is worth the drive." She's over the moon.
Cold enough to see your breath this morning.
First one of the season. Scraped the windshield with a spatula.
A spatula?! Where's the ice scraper?
That's a summer Jim problem. Summer Jim didn't prepare.
The gym added a sauna.
Okay, NOW I'll renew the membership.
That's all it took? A hot wooden box?
I think the parakeet learned to imitate the microwave.
The beep?
The beep. I've checked for phantom leftovers six times today.
Game night got moved to our place.
How many people?
Ten. Maybe twelve.
I'll get the folding chairs. And hide the good glasses.
The peaches are two for a dollar!
That's a make-a-pie kind of price.
Exactly what I was thinking. Grab a basket.
He fixed the squeaky door!
After two years?
Two years, one hinge, thirty seconds of oil. I can't discuss it further.
Traffic report says the tunnel's backed up to the exit.
Surface streets it is.
Add fifteen minutes, but at least we're moving.
Grandpa beat everyone at the crossword. Again.
He's ninety-one!
Ninety-one and merciless. He did it in pen.
Wait, the store closes at nine, right?
Eight on Sundays.
It's 7:40. RUN.
My sister named the baby after grandma.
Oh, that's lovely. Little Rosemarie.
Grandma cried for an hour. Happy crying, the good kind.
Anyone else smell burning?
The toast! I forgot the toast!
That's the third slice today. The toaster's winning.
The playoffs start Thursday. I've cleared my calendar.
The whole calendar?
Doctor's appointment and all. Priorities.
They repainted the fence without asking us. It's OUR fence.
What color?
That's the thing... it looks amazing.
So are we mad or grateful? Pick a lane.
The bakery ran out of bagels by eight.
By EIGHT? What kind of operation are they running?
A popular one, apparently.
I can hear your stomach from here.
Lunch was a granola bar and optimism.
There's chili in the fridge. Go. Eat.
We won the office fantasy league!
"We"? You benched my guy every week!
And we still won, which proves my point.
The moving truck's here early.
How early?
They're-carrying-the-couch-right-now early. Get up!
Doctor says I need reading glasses.
Welcome to the club. We meet wherever we left our glasses.
So... nowhere, ever?
Exactly. You're catching on already.
Fresh snow on the mountain — first tracks tomorrow?
Alarm's set for five. Don't you dare hit snooze.
One snooze. As a treat.
The tooth fairy forgot last night.
Oh no. Code red. What's the plan?
"She was delayed by weather." Two dollars for the inconvenience.
Inflation hits everyone, huh.
My book club actually finished the book this month.
All of you?
Well. Three of us. But that's a record!
Someone put a free piano on the curb on Maple.
A real one? Does it work?
Mostly? Two keys stick.
Do not bring home the piano. Do NOT bring home—
Beach or mountains for the long weekend?
Mountains. My sunburn from last time still has feelings.
Cabins it is. I'll check the one with the wood stove.
You're humming. Why are you humming?
Got the last parking spot AND the elevator was waiting. It's a good day.
Chips or pretzels for the road trip?
Both. It's a four-hour drive; we're not animals.
The dentist has a cancellation at two. Want it?
Today? Ugh... yes. Fine. Yes.
My plants survived the whole vacation!
All of them?
Well, the fern is "resting." But the rest, yes!
It's drizzling. Do we still run?
Drizzle isn't rain. Lace up.
You're impossible. Two miles, max.
Did you hear the coyotes last night?
That's what that was?! I thought someone's TV was up loud.
Nope. Nature. Right behind the cul-de-sac.
The dryer ate another sock.
Same pair as last time?
Its partner, actually. The dryer is a poet.
I'm making dad's chili for the cook-off.
The one with the secret ingredient?
Cocoa powder. If I win, that stays between us.
My phone says the train's delayed twelve minutes.
Coffee, then?
Twist my arm. The place with the good muffins.
She said her first word!
What was it?!
"Uh-oh." Perfectly timed, too — she dropped her spoon first.
The neighbors' sprinklers run at noon. Noon!
In this heat? Half of it evaporates before it lands.
I keep almost saying something. Almost.
Popcorn's ready — what are we watching?
The baking show. No arguments.
The baking show was ALWAYS the plan.
My knees predicted this rain two days ago.
Your knees are better than the weather app.
Cheaper subscription, too.
The market had fresh figs today. Figs!
You bought all of them, didn't you.
Define "all."
Can you believe he still hasn't seen the ocean?
He's thirty!
Thirty. So — road trip this summer? We fix this?
We fix this. I'm in.
The office is doing a chili cook-off Friday.
Wait, you AND Deb are entering?
It's war, and my beans are ready.
I found a twenty in my winter coat!
The universe's finest gift.
Lunch is on me. Well — on the coat.
He wants to camp in the backyard tonight.
It's supposed to drop to forty.
Extra sleeping bag, hot chocolate, and I give him till midnight.
Generous. I say ten thirty.
What's the score?
You don't want to know.
That bad?
Turn on literally anything else.
The escalator's out at the station again.
Which one?
The long one. The forty-five-second one. My legs are still filing complaints.
Grandma's coming for a whole month!
A month! Where's she sleeping?
The office becomes her kingdom. We move the desk tonight.
I tried that stretching video you sent.
The twenty-minute one?
I lasted eight. My hamstrings sent a formal protest.
Pizza place changed their crust.
No.
Yes. It's... fluffier?
Fluffier is not what I signed up for.
Your tomato plants are showing off again.
Twelve on one vine this week!
Okay, now you're just bragging. Bring some over.
The parade rerouted right past our street!
So we can watch from the porch?
Porch seats, hot cider, front row. Best year ever.
I keep hearing dripping but I can't find it.
Under the sink?
Checked. Bathroom, checked. It's a ghost with a faucet.
The team traded our best player.
They WHAT.
For two draft picks and "future considerations."
I need to sit down. This is a betrayal.
Homemade or store-bought for the bake sale?
When have I ever brought store-bought?
The muffin incident of 2022.
We agreed never to speak of that.
My uncle carved a bear out of a tree stump.
On purpose?
Chainsaw and everything. It's honestly... pretty good?
The crosswalk finally got a signal!
After all those petitions?
Three years of letters. The kids can walk to school by themselves now.
Ooh, the cider stand is back!
Since when?
This morning! First batch of fall — still warm.
He organized the spice rack alphabetically.
That's... sweet?
Allspice to za'atar. He seems so at peace. I'm not touching anything.
Flu shots at the pharmacy, no appointment.
Both of us, lunch break?
Deal. Ice cream after; I make the rules.
The kitten found the yarn drawer.
How bad?
The living room looks like a laser museum heist.
Is that thunder or the truck again?
Thunder. Look at those clouds rolling in.
Windows! I left the windows open!
I got us a reservation at the rooftop place.
The one with the view? How?
Cancellation. Seven o'clock — wear the nice shoes.
My mile time's under nine minutes now!
From eleven? That's huge!
Six months of hill repeats. My lungs earned this.
We're out of coffee.
Don't joke about that.
The bag is EMPTY, Dana.
Get your coat. This is an emergency run.
The twins' recital is Sunday at four.
Piano or the kazoo phase?
Piano, thankfully. The kazoo phase has passed.
We'll never forget it, though. We CAN'T.
The lake path floods every spring, and every spring we forget.
How wet are your shoes?
Completely. Fully. Spiritually.
Someone's selling fresh eggs three doors down.
The rooster house?
The rooster house. At least he's contributing to society now.
I finally watched the finale.
AND?
We need dinner and two hours. Maybe three.
The heat broke! It's actually breezy!
Windows open, oven on. I'm baking everything we own.
It's the first cool day, not the last — pace yourself!
Dad joined a bowling league.
His backswing is a public hazard.
They gave him a nickname already. "Thunder."
"Thunder." I can never unknow this.
Toll went up again on the bridge.
Again?! It was two fifty last year.
Three seventy-five now. The ferry's looking better every day.
I pickled the extra cucumbers.
How many jars?
Eleven.
ELEVEN? We're a family of three!
The barber gave him the exact haircut from the photo.
Wait, the photo of YOU in college?
The mullet lives again, and honestly, he's pulling it off.
My sister's flight got in early for once.
Early! Is she already at the house?
Sitting on the porch with snacks like she owns the place.
The garden gnome is missing.
Missing or "missing"?
There's a ransom note. It's the Hendersons' kids. It's adorable.
Pay the ransom. Two popsicles, was it?
Farm stand corn is in. Summer's officially real.
First dozen tonight?
Already in the pot. Bring butter.
You seen my headphones?
Kitchen counter, next to the bananas.
The muffins are done — catch one while they're warm!
Blueberry?
Is there any other kind worth making?
Fog's so thick I can't see the mailbox.
Drive slow, okay? Lights on, the whole deal.
He napped through the entire fireworks show.
Through the finale too?!
Didn't even twitch. That child could sleep through a marching band.
Ten bucks says it snows before Halloween.
You said that last year.
And I was one week off. ONE week.
The soup place changed owners.
Is the tomato bisque safe?
Same recipe, same cook. We can all breathe.
My badge wouldn't scan and the whole line watched me fail.
The walk of shame to the security desk?
Every step of it.
She grew two inches over the summer!
The pediatrician's sure? Two whole inches?
Marked it on the doorframe myself. The pencil doesn't lie.
Last night's leftovers or grilled cheese?
You had me at grilled cheese.
The car wash gave me a free upgrade.
The wax thing?
The wax thing! She's gleaming. I parked far away on purpose.
My bracket's busted already.
It's the first weekend!
Tell that to the twelve-seed that ruined my life.
Puddles everywhere and he wore his new sneakers.
Did he jump in?
Both feet. Zero hesitation. Full commitment.
I told the barber "just a trim."
That is NOT a trim.
It'll grow back. I'm telling myself it'll grow back.
The night market opens this weekend.
The one with the dumpling stall?
The very one. We're going hungry — no snacks before.
Your phone's been buzzing nonstop.
The group chat. Someone said the wrong thing about pineapple pizza.
Say no more. Godspeed.
The hardware store guy remembered our deck project.
From April?!
Asked how the railing turned out and everything. Legend.
It hailed for exactly two minutes.
The garden okay?
Lettuce took casualties. The tomatoes fought bravely.
He's teaching the dog to skateboard.
The dog or himself?
At this point the dog's got better balance.
Bus was so full I stood the whole way.
The whole forty minutes?
Somebody's backpack was in my spine the entire ride.
The dishwasher's fixed!
What was it?
A single popcorn kernel. Eighty dollars.
Eighty dollars per kernel. Incredible economy.
I bought too many plums. Again.
Plum jam weekend?
Plum jam weekend.
Frost got the basil.
Not the basil!
I salvaged enough for one last pesto. We eat like kings tonight, then we mourn.
The kid across the street set up a hot cocoa stand.
In this weather? Genius.
Fifty cents a cup and a line down the block. I respect the hustle.
My debit card got declined at the bakery. So embarrassing.
The bank froze it?
"Suspicious activity." The suspicious activity was buying croissants two days in a row.
He finally cleaned the gutters.
Voluntarily?
The ladder's still against the house, so let's not celebrate yet.
The train app crashed right when I needed it.
Which platform did you gamble on?
Wrong one. Obviously.
Karaoke night — you in?
Only if you do the duet.
I ALWAYS do the duet. That's not a real condition.
Someone brought donuts to the meeting.
Someone is a hero.
Someone was me.
Someone is a hero AND humble.
My sunflowers are taller than the fence now.
Taller than YOU?
Taller than me, the fence, and my pride combined.
I fell asleep in the hammock all afternoon.
In October?
Best nap of my adult life. Woke up under two blankets someone kindly deployed.
We're doing secret santa this year — ten dollar limit.
Ten? That's a candle or half a candle.
Get creative. Last year someone gifted a haiku.
The intersection got a roundabout.
Oh no. Half this town can't handle a four-way stop.
It's day one and I've already seen three people just... give up and orbit.
My tooth's been aching since the caramel apple.
Call the dentist.
It might settle down—
The caramel apple already won. Call the dentist.
Kite festival's Sunday!
Windy enough, you think?
The forecast says fifteen knots. Perfect kite math.
He alphabetized my bookshelf as a "surprise."
It was sorted by color!
It took me a YEAR to do the rainbow.
The stew's better today than yesterday.
Day-two stew is a scientific fact.
Then day-three stew tomorrow should be transcendent.
Lost my gloves. Second pair this winter.
Get the ones with the clip.
The clip is for children.
The clip is for people who keep their gloves.
The neighbor's kid made varsity!
Freshman year?!
Freshman year. His dad's already bought the foam finger.
My luggage went to the wrong city.
Which city got lucky?
Denver. My socks are in Denver.
The bagel place knows us as "the Tuesday couple."
We go on Tuesdays ONE time— okay, every Tuesday.
Own it. We're regulars. We've made it.
The porch light's been flickering all week.
Bulb or wiring?
Bulb, I hope. Wiring means calling your brother.
He still talks about the ceiling fan. Please let it be the bulb.
Two inches of rain in an hour, they said.
The basement?
Dry so far. The new gutter's earning its keep.
I made the team!
The over-forty league?
Second base, baby! First practice is Tuesday.
Ice your everything afterward. Trust me.
Movie tickets are how much now?!
Plus the popcorn mortgage.
Matinee. We're matinee people now.
The fair added a giant slide this year.
The burlap sack kind?
The burlap sack kind. I don't care how old we are — we're doing it.
Aunt Mei sent a care package.
The good noodles?
The good noodles, three kinds of tea, and socks. She's the best.
My interview got moved up to tomorrow.
Tomorrow?! You ready?
Suit's ironed, answers rehearsed... mostly. Quiz me at dinner?
The crickets are LOUD tonight.
That means summer's almost done, you know.
Don't say that. We still have a whole camping trip left.
Found the missing remote.
Where?
Freezer.
The FREEZER? No — actually, don't explain. Some mysteries are better left alone.
The recipe made twelve and the pan holds eight.
So... four extra batter?
Four extra for the chef. That's just baking law.
Your umbrella's dripping on my shoes.
Better my umbrella than the actual sky, which is what got me.
Third snooze alarm. THIRD.
Okay, okay — I'm up. Technically.
He grew a mustache over the long weekend.
And? Verdict?
The jury's out. The jury may be out for some time.
Any bites at the garage sale?
Sold the treadmill in ten minutes. The bread machine? Crickets.
The cat's staring at the wall again.
Same spot?
Same spot. Either it's a spider or we have a ghost with boring taste.
I meal-prepped for the whole week!
It's Wednesday and the fridge is empty.
The prep was delicious. The plan had flaws.
Two seats left on the earlier flight.
Grab them! I'll pack tonight.
My old roommate is in town Friday.
Trivia night reunion?
Already texted the team. The dynasty returns.
The office switched to standing desks.
How are your feet?
My feet have opinions. Loud ones.
Lemonade stand or the sprinkler — the kids can't decide.
It's ninety degrees. Sprinkler first, lemonade after. Everyone wins.
The mechanic said it's just a loose belt.
So not the four-hundred-dollar noise you feared?
Forty dollars. I nearly hugged him.
My mom liked your casserole better than hers.
She SAID that?
Out loud. In front of witnesses.
The trail's closed for bear activity.
Bear activity?! Which trail?
The one we hiked LAST WEEKEND.
The kid lost his first tooth at school.
Did he cry?
He charged admission to see the gap. A quarter per look.
Ripe avocados. Six of them. Today only.
Guacamole emergency protocols. I'll get chips.
The choir needs another tenor.
Don't look at me.
You sing in the shower every single morning.
The shower has no auditions.
The power flickered and the oven clock's blinking again.
Just leave it. It's been 12:00 since March anyway.
He walked the dog in his robe again.
It's noon!
The man is living his best life, honestly.
The bakery's out of the cinnamon rolls.
Since WHEN?
Since some tour bus stopped by at seven. A tour bus, Karen.
My stepcount is embarrassing today.
Number?
Four hundred. It's 6 p.m.
Walk with me to the mailbox. Baby steps. Literally.
The window guy quoted us half of what the first one did.
Half?! What's the catch?
No catch — he's the first guy's former apprentice. Awkward and wonderful.
I planted garlic today. See you in July, garlic.
Nine months for garlic?
Good things take time. Garlic knows this.
The quiz said which pasta shape I am.
And?
Rigatoni. "Sturdy and dependable." I've never felt so seen.
Rain delayed the game two hours.
Did you stay?
Under a poncho with cold nachos. Loyalty has a price.
My phone fell between the car seats.
The Bermuda Gap.
Whole arm in, shoulder deep, found two fries and a crayon first.
The neighbor loaned us his snowblower!
The fancy one with headlights?
Headlights and heated grips. I did the whole block by nine.
She wants a unicorn cake for her birthday.
You can barely make toast.
Which is why YOU'RE getting a very flattering phone call right now.
Book fair week. Send help and small bills.
How many books came home last year?
Fourteen. Two were about the same shark.
The carpool playlist needs new songs.
Whose turn to pick?
Yours — and if it's all sea shanties again, you're walking.
Someone shoveled our walk before we woke up.
The Hendersons?
Had to be. I'm baking them the thank-you banana bread.
My tea's gone cold twice today.
Microwave it, coward.
Some of us have STANDARDS.
Cold standards, apparently.
The parade threw candy right onto our blanket.
Jackpot spot?
Best real estate in town. We defend it with our lives next year.
My brother's growing hot peppers now.
The man who cries at mild salsa?
Character development. Or hubris. We'll know by August.
The overnight oats worked!
So breakfast is just... waiting there? Every morning?
Like tiny cold porridge elves prepared it. I'm never going back.
The kids found my old records.
Do they get it?
They think "rewinding" the record is a feature. I need to lie down.
Vet bill was less than the estimate.
How much less?
Enough for the good dog treats AND dinner out. Everyone wins.
Frost on the pumpkin this morning.
Real frost or picturesque frost?
Both. I took nine photos of the same pumpkin.
The wedding invite says "festive attire."
Which means...?
Nobody knows. NOBODY knows. That's the problem.
Our street made the paper — biggest maple in the county!
The one at the corner?
Front page, full color. The tree's basically a celebrity now.
Grandma double-booked her birthday parties.
Both sides of the family?
Same day, opposite ends of town. She says she's "in demand."
The soup exchange is back on at church.
What are we making?
The minestrone. We have a title to defend.
My haircut's too short but it'll be fine in two weeks.
That's what you always say.
And in two weeks, I'm always right.
The ice cream truck changed its song!
You can't change the song. That's the whole—
It plays something jazzy now. The children are confused. I'm confused.
He fixed the wobbly table with a folded napkin.
The engineer? THE engineer?
Four degrees, and his solution was a napkin. I married a genius.
The last tomato of the season.
Ceremony?
Sliced, salt, a little olive oil. We eat it on the porch at sunset like civilized people.
The gym buddy system works — I actually went.
How many days straight?
Four. FOUR. There's a chart on my fridge and everything.
My library holds all arrived at once.
How many?
Seven. Seven books, three weeks. Cancel my plans.
The blackout lasted four hours.
What'd you do?
Candles, board games, and the good chocolate we were "saving." Honestly? Great night.
The dog and the mailman are friends now.
Since when?
Since the treats started. Betrayal by biscuit, they call it.
First lightning bugs of the summer tonight.
Already?
Caught one, made a wish, let it go. Same as every year since we were six.
Did the standup get moved again?
Yeah, ten thirty now. Third time this week.
Ugh. Why do we even have a calendar?
So the deadline's Friday.
Wait — this Friday? Like, four days from now?
Yep. Found out an hour ago.
Oh no. Oh no no no.
Hey, did you ship the fix?
Shipped it last night. Fingers crossed.
The client wants three more revisions.
Three?! We already did five!
I know. I know. Just... breathe.
I'm gonna skip the all-hands.
You can't — they're announcing the reorg.
Wait, what reorg?
Boss said "quick sync" and it ran ninety minutes.
Classic. Was there even an agenda?
There was a vibe. No agenda.
Can you cover my one-on-one tomorrow?
Hmm, I've got the vendor call at two...
It's at four.
Oh — then sure, easy.
Sprint review went okay, I think?
You think? Marco looked furious.
That's just his face, honestly.
We're behind on the roadmap again.
By how much this time?
Six weeks. Maybe seven.
Whew. Okay. That's... a lot.
The demo crashed in front of everyone.
Oh no. What did you do?
Smiled and said "imagine it working."
Quick question — is the offsite still on?
As far as I know, yeah. Why?
I may have double-booked myself.
They moved retro to Monday morning.
Monday?! Who reflects on a Monday?
Apparently we do now.
I need those slides by noon.
Noon today or noon tomorrow?
Today. Sorry.
You're killing me, Priya.
Honestly, that meeting could've been an email.
An email? It could've been nothing.
Do we have a room booked for the review?
I grabbed the small one — the fishbowl.
The one with the broken screen?
...I'll rebook.
My calendar is back-to-back till five.
Skip something. Seriously.
I can't, they're all with the VP.
Then bring snacks and good luck.
The launch slipped again, didn't it.
It did. Two more weeks.
I'm not even surprised anymore.
Okay so, status check — where are we on the report?
Draft's done; charts are... in progress.
In progress meaning?
Meaning I haven't started them.
Someone scheduled a meeting at 8 a.m.
On purpose? That's hostile.
Right? I'm declining it.
Guess who volunteered us for the audit.
No. Don't say it.
Dave. Dave said it.
I finally cleared my inbox!
All of it? Like, zero unread?
Zero! I feel unstoppable.
Give it an hour.
Are you presenting first or am I?
You open, I close — deal?
Deal. Don't run long this time.
The stakeholders keep changing the scope.
Again? What is it now?
Now they want it in French too.
In French. Sure. Why not.
Heads up, the review got pushed to Thursday.
Oh thank goodness. I needed that.
Did anyone take notes in that meeting?
I thought you were taking notes.
I thought Sam was!
Sam wasn't even there.
